system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet straight duct (along +x)
    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (  0  25 -25)   // 1
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 3
    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5 (same as 1)
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6 (same as 2)
    (-50 -25  25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend roof (connects block 0 to bend)
    (  0  25  25)   // 8 (same as 4)
    (  0  25 -25)   // 9 (same as 5/1)
    ( 25  25 -25)   // 10
    ( 25  25  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - bend wall (goes down in -y)
    (  0 -25  25)   // 12 (same as 7)
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 13 (same as 6/2)
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 15

    // Block 3 - outlet straight duct (extending in -y)
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 16 (same as 15)
    ( 25 -125  25)   // 17
    ( 25 -125 -25)   // 18
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 19 (same as 14)
    (-500 -50  25)   // 20
    (-500 -50 -25)  // 21
    (-500 -125 -25) // 22
    (-500 -125  25) // 23
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight duct (along +x)
    // Bottom face (0 3 2 1) viewed from +z: CCW => outward normal +z
    // Top face (4 7 6 5) directly above
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend roof connecting block 0 to bend
    // Bottom face (4 5 9 8) viewed from +z: CCW => outward normal +z
    // Top face (8 9 10 11) at y=25 plane
    hex (4 5 9 8 8 9 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Bend wall going down in -y
    // Bottom face (12 13 14 15) viewed from +z: CCW => outward normal +z
    // Top face (15 14 10 11) at y=-50 plane
    hex (12 13 14 15 15 14 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Outlet straight duct extending in -y
    // Bottom face (16 19 18 17) viewed from +z: CCW => outward normal +z
    // Top face (17 18 22 23) at y=-125 plane
    hex (16 19 18 17 17 18 22 23) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (23 22 18 17)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 1 5 4)   // front wall (+z)
            (0 3 2 1)   // back wall (-z)
            (1 2 6 5)   // left wall (-y)
            (4 5 6 7)   // right wall (+y)

            // Block 1 walls
            (8 9 10 11) // front wall (+z)
            (8 11 9 10) // back wall (-z)
            (4 8 11 7)  // left wall (-y)
            (5 6 10 9)  // right wall (+y)

            // Block 2 walls
            (12 13 14 15) // front wall (+z)
            (12 15 14 13) // back wall (-z)
            (12 13 19 16) // left wall (-y)
            (15 14 10 11) // right wall (+y)

            // Block 3 walls
            (16 17 22 18) // front wall (+z)
            (16 18 22 19) // back wall (-z)
            (16 17 23 19) // left wall (-y)
            (19 22 22 18) // right wall (+y)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are still two errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (4 5 9 8 8 9 10 11) ... has inward-pointing faces`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Error 1 Deep Dive

The error is:
```
Block hex (4 5 9 8 8 9 10 11) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(8 11 10 9) 4(8 9 10 11)
```

The block definition `hex (4 5 9 8 8 9 10 11)` creates a degenerate/hollow block because vertices 8 and 9 are repeated. This happens because the bend region tries to connect blocks using shared vertices, but the vertex ordering creates a collapsed/inverted hexahedron.

### Root Cause Analysis

Looking at the vertex coordinates:
- Vertex 4: (0, 25, 25)
- Vertex 5: (0, 25, -25)
- Vertex 8: (0, 25, 25) ← SAME AS VERTEX 4
- Vertex 9: (0, 25, -25) ← SAME AS VERTEX 5
- Vertex 10: (25, 25, -25)
- Vertex 11: (25, 25, 25)

Vertices 4 and 8 are identical, and vertices 5 and 9 are identical. This means the "bottom face" `(4 5 9 8)` collapses into a line segment rather than a quadrilateral. No vertex ordering will fix a degenerate block — the geometry fundamentally doesn't support three separate blocks sharing the bend face.

### Recommended Solution

The block topology needs to be redesigned. The fundamental problem is that the current approach tries to make three blocks share the bend face, but vertices 4/8 and 5/9 being coincident means the middle block has zero thickness.

Instead, **use a simpler 2-block approach**:

1. **Remove Block 1** entirely (the bend roof block). Connect Block 0 directly to Block 2 through the shared edge at x=0, y=±25.
2. **Redefine Block 2** to include the full L-shaped path. The block should span from x=-50..0 and y=-50..25 (covering the corner region).
3. **Redefine Block 3** as the long outlet duct from y=-50 to y=-125 at x=25.

Specifically, the blocks should be:

- **Block 0** (inlet duct): `hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5)` — the straight duct along +x
- **Block 1** (corner/bend): `hex (4 5 10 11 7 6 13 12)` — spanning x=0..25, y=-25..25 (this replaces the old Block 1 and part of Block 2)
- **Block 2** (post-bend duct): `hex (12 13 15 14 16 19 18 17)` — the outlet duct along -y

But even this may have issues. The cleanest approach is to **copy the exact blockMeshDict from the squareBend tutorial verbatim**. The tutorial already contains a working multi-block decomposition for a square bend. Simply replace the contents of your `blockMeshDict`'s `blocks` and `boundary` sections with the tutorial's proven definitions, keeping the same vertex coordinates (0-23).

Key principles for the tutorial-style blocks:
- Each hex must have 8 distinct vertices forming a convex parallelepiped
- The right-hand rule must apply to both the bottom face `(0 1 2 3)` and top face `(4 5 6 7)`
- Boundary faces must be consistent with the final block adjacency

## Error 2

This is a cascading error. Once blockMesh succeeds, this resolves automatically.

## Summary

1. **Redesign the block topology** in `blockMeshDict` to avoid degenerate/hollow blocks. Remove the problematic Block 1 and restructure Blocks 2 and 3 so that no block has duplicate/reused vertices causing collapse. A 2-block or 3-block configuration where each hex has 8 unique vertices is essential.

2. **All boundary face definitions** in `blockMeshDict` must be updated to match the new block topology.

3. **Re-run `blockMesh`** before attempting `rhoSimpleFoam`.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Update all boundary face definitions to match new block topology: inlet gets face (0 4 7 3), outlet gets face (20 23 22 21), walls get appropriate faces from each block ensuring no face is listed twice')]
